nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Mortimer was born about 1295, in Norfolk, England, his father, Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk, was 40 and his mother, Lucy de la Wafre, was 37. He had at least 2 sons with Margery Fastolf. He died on 25 September 1387, at the age of 93, and was buried in Attleborough, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Mortimer est né environ 1295, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Lucy de la Wafre, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Margery Fastolf. Il est décédé le 25 septembre 1387, à l’âge de 93 ans, et a été enterré à Attleborough, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Mortimer um 1295 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk 40 und seine Mutter Lucy de la Wafre 37. Er hatte mit Margery Fastolf mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 25. September 1387 im Alter von 93 Jahren und wurde in Attleborough, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Mortimer è nato il circa 1295 nel Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Lucy de la Wafre, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Margery Fastolf. Morì il 25 settembre 1387, all'età di 93 anni, e fu sepolto a Attleborough, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Mortimerが約1295年にイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirkは40歳，母Lucy de la Wafreは37歳であった。彼はMargery Fastolfとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1387年9月25日に93歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクアットレボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Mortimer(이)가 대략 1295년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucy de la Wafre(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Margery Fastolf(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1387년 9월 25일 93세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 애틀레버루그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Mortimer nasceu aproximadamente 1295, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Lucy de la Wafre, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Margery Fastolf. Ele faleceu em 25 de setembro de 1387, com 93 anos, e foi sepultado em Attleborough, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Mortimer родился около 1295 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucy de la Wafre, 37. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Margery Fastolf. Он умер 25 сентября 1387 г. в возрасте 93 лет и был похоронен в местности аттлебороую, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Mortimer nació aproximadamente 1295, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Mortimer Baron of Chirk, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Lucy de la Wafre, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Margery Fastolf. Murió el 25 de septiembre de 1387, a la edad de 93 años, y fue sepultado en Attleborough, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
